commonProblems.h2: Kia Sedona Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Kia Sedona is a dependable large MPV, but its high-mileage petrol V6 units develop specific weaknesses that frequently drive owners toward complete engine replacement. Replacement demand is heavily concentrated on the 3.5-litre G6EA MPI and 3.3-litre G6DH GDI Lambda V6 engines, both of which suffer from interference designs and complex thermal failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EnginesMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the tiered cost breakdowns below as a decision tool to determine whether a targeted repair or a full rebuilt Kia Sedona engine replacement delivers the best long-term value for your vehicle.

problems.h4: Kia Sedona Head Gasket Failure - Why 3.3 GDI Engines Strip Head Bolts
problems.affectedModels: Kia Sedona LX, SE, SR, SX, Platinum (2015-2021, G6DH 3.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: The G6DH engine uses a head bolt thread design prone to stripping from the aluminium block under repeated thermal cycling, causing catastrophic compression loss and coolant migration into the crankcase.

repairOptions.tier: Major Repair - Cylinder Head Refit & Thread Repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Machining the block deck, installing helicoil/thread inserts, and resealing the head with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block threads are partially intact. High recurrence risk within 15,000 miles on weakened aluminium.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of a complete rebuilt G6DH unit featuring reinforced block threads, upgraded gasket set, and bench-tested oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Permanently resolves cylinder compression and coolant sealing. Backed by a 12-month warranty with components engineered to exceed original TSB specifications.

problems.recommendation: On a Kia Sedona with confirmed head bolt stripping, a standard Kia Sedona head gasket replacement will almost always fail due to compromised block integrity. We strongly recommend a full rebuilt Kia Sedona engine replacement with updated thread reinforcement, as it permanently resolves the factory flaw and protects your long-term investment in the vehicle.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt G6DH Kia Sedona engine prices now

problems.h4: Kia Sedona Timing Chain Failure - P0018 Code on 3.5 V6 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Kia Sedona LX, SE, SR, EX (2006-2014, G6EA 3.5L V6)
problems.rootCause: The primary timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure or guide rails wear prematurely, allowing the chain to jump teeth and trigger P0018 camshaft correlation faults. Because this is an interference engine, valve-to-piston contact follows immediately.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Timing Chain Kit & Guide Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing tensioners, rails, and primary chain assembly with updated components. Does not repair bent valves.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before valve contact occurs. Once P0018 or P0300 codes appear, hidden top-end damage is highly likely.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete Kia Sedona engine swap with rebuilt cylinder head, new valve train, updated tensioners, and full gasket installation
repairOptions.longevity: Guarantees correct valve timing and restored factory compression. Includes upgraded timing components to prevent premature stretch.

problems.recommendation: If your Kia Sedona is displaying a P0018 code or sudden power loss, do not delay. The 3.5L interference design means a jumped chain typically destroys valves and pistons instantly. We recommend a full rebuilt Kia Sedona engine replacement rather than attempting a partial timing kit repair, as it eliminates hidden mechanical damage and provides a comprehensive specialist warranty.
problems.cta: Get quotes for G6EA Kia Sedona timing chain failure replacement

problems.group: Kia Sedona Metal Flakes in Oil
problems.h4: Kia Sedona Metal Flakes in Oil - Lambda II Bottom End Wear
problems.affectedModels: Kia Sedona SE, SX, Platinum (2015-2021, G6DH 3.3L V6 GDI)
problems.rootCause: Extended service intervals and marginal oil pressure at high RPM accelerate wear on the crankshaft rod bearings, releasing ferrous shavings into the sump that gradually contaminate the main oil galleries.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Bearing Replacement & Oil Gallery Flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dropping the sump, replacing upper/lower rod bearings, and performing a high-pressure oil system flush to remove contaminants
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses early-stage wear. If debris has entered the main oil pump or scored cylinder walls, recurrence is inevitable.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement featuring a balanced crankshaft, upgraded high-flow oil pump, and precision-matched bearing clearances
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates metal contamination risks entirely. The rebuilt unit features upgraded bearing alloys designed for extended drain intervals and thermal stability.

problems.recommendation: Once metallic particles appear on your Kia Sedona magnetic drain plug, progressive lubrication failure is already underway. A simple Kia Sedona bearing replacement rarely clears the contaminated oil pump or gallery deposits. We advise choosing a full rebuilt Kia Sedona engine to guarantee clean oil passages and upgraded bearing durability, avoiding costly repeat failures.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt Kia Sedona 3.3 V6 bearing failure prices

problems.h4: Kia Sedona P0300 Misfire - Coolant Loss and Head Warping
problems.affectedModels: Kia Sedona 3.5 V6, 3.3 GDI (2006-2021, G6EA & G6DH)
problems.rootCause: Failing thermostat housings and degraded water pumps cause intermittent overheating cycles, leading to cylinder head warping, coolant seepage into combustion chambers, and persistent random misfire codes across multiple cylinders.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Cooling System Overhaul & Head Reseal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing water pump, thermostat, hoses, and performing a pressure test with head gasket reseal using reinforced bolts
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for minor leaks and early-stage warping under 0.1mm. Unsuitable if hydrolock or cylinder scoring has already occurred.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of a completely rebuilt V6 with machined cylinder head, upgraded cooling gaskets, and high-efficiency water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Permanently resolves overheating history and misfire codes. The rebuilt unit includes a pressure-tested cooling system and a 12-month warranty.

problems.recommendation: If your Kia Sedona P0300 codes coincide with sweet-smelling exhaust or consistent coolant loss, the head has likely warped beyond safe reseal limits. A minor Kia Sedona cooling overhaul is only worthwhile if a professional flatness test confirms minimal deformation. Otherwise, we recommend a full rebuilt Kia Sedona engine replacement, which guarantees correct thermal management and eliminates persistent misfire risks.
problems.cta: Compare Kia Sedona P0300 misfire repair and replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Kia Sedona
finalCta.paragraph: The large V6 engines in the Kia Sedona deliver reliable family hauling capability, but when critical failures like head bolt stripping, timing chain jumps, or progressive bearing wear occur, the repair bills can quickly approach or exceed the vehicle's market value. EnginesMarket removes the uncertainty by connecting you directly with vetted UK specialists who supply rebuilt units specifically calibrated to outlast the original factory weaknesses. Every replacement comes with upgraded internal components, rigorous bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, with flexible supply-and-fit options and nationwide courier delivery available across the UK. Compare prices today and secure a dependable, dealer-quality solution without paying the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Kia Sedona Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Kia Sedona and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Kia Sedona Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to the UK Kia Sedona is listed below with compatible codes, production years and common failure notes to ensure you source the exact replacement unit.

items.title: Kia Sedona 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Appearing exclusively in the second generation (2008-2014), this 2.2‑litre CRDi diesel was introduced to provide improved fuel economy and higher torque for fleet operators and high‑mileage family haulers. Utilising common‑rail direct injection and a variable geometry turbocharger, it served as a niche alternative to the dominant petrol V6 units but remains sought after for long‑distance towing efficiency.
commonFailurePoints: injector seal failures at high mileage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Kia Sedona 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Kia Sedona 3.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: Serving as the standard powerplant for the third generation (2015-2021), the 3.3‑litre Lambda II GDI V6 marked a significant shift toward direct injection technology to meet stricter emissions standards without sacrificing the MPV’s towing and acceleration capabilities. As the most searched replacement unit in the UK Sedona network, it powers all trim levels but requires careful attention to known factory design flaws affecting long‑term reliability.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head bolt thread stripping causing catastrophic head gasket failure
followed by progressive rod bearing wear
items.cta: Compare Kia Sedona 3.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Kia Sedona 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The backbone of the second generation (2006-2014) and late first generation models, the 3.5‑litre DOHC V6 utilises multi‑port injection to deliver smooth, predictable power across a wide rev range. Known for its robust bottom end when properly maintained, this displacement dominates older UK Sedona registrations and requires specific diagnostic checks for timing system wear before considering partial repairs.
commonFailurePoints: Primary timing chain tensioner collapse leading to P0018 correlation faults
items.cta: Compare Kia Sedona 3.5L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Kia Sedona needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Kia Sedona specialists.

families: G6DH - 3.3L Lambda II GDI V6 (2015-2021), head bolt stripping and metal flakes in oil are documented failure patterns
G6EA - 3.5L DOHC MPI V6 (2006-2014), timing chain tensioner collapse triggers P0018 correlation faults
G6CU - 3.8L early V6 (1999-2005), largely superseded but still sought for first-generation restorations

Second Generation VQ/YP (2006-2014) - G6EA 3.5L MPI V6, G6CU 3.8L early units
knownFor: Smooth, refined power delivery ideal for MPV family hauling and motorway cruising

G6EA timing chain jump on interference design risks bent valves without warning
Metal flakes in oil indicating progressive rod bearing wear on high-mileage Lambda II units

items.description: No hybrid, plug-in hybrid, mild hybrid or pure electric variants of the Kia Sedona were officially sold in the UK market across any generation.

engineYears.h2: Kia Sedona Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers every major engine change across the Kia Sedona's UK production run from 1999 to 2021, organised so owners can quickly identify their exact engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices from vetted UK specialists.

years.preview: First Generation Launch & Early V6 Units
years.description: The original Sedona debuted with naturally aspirated MPI V6 petrol engines, establishing the large MPV format but with limited UK diesel availability.
keyChanges: First-generation Sedona launched in UK with 3.8L and early 3.5L V6 petrol options
No factory diesel variant officially offered in UK market during this period
Basic OBD-II diagnostics, minimal electronic engine management complexity
knownFor: Simple MPI design offers straightforward diagnostics and affordable rebuilds
Timing belt replacement critical at 60k miles to avoid catastrophic interference damage
High-mileage units often suffer oil consumption from valve stem seal wear
Limited UK parts availability drives demand for verified replacement engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2005 Kia Sedona replacement engines
years.ticker: Kia Sedona 3.8 engine replacement • G6CU timing belt kit • first generation Sedona V6 for sale
enquiries: first generation Sedona V6 for sale

years.preview: Second Generation Launch: G6EA 3.5L V6 Dominates
years.description: The all-new YP platform introduced the refined 3.5L DOHC V6 with improved emissions and the rare 2.2L CRDi diesel option for UK fleet buyers.
keyChanges: Second-generation Sedona launched with updated styling and improved crash safety
G6EA 3.5L DOHC V6 became the standard petrol engine across all UK trims
Optional 2.2L D4HB CRDi diesel introduced for high-mileage commercial users
mainEngines: D4HB - 2.2L diesel (SE CRDi, SR CRDi - limited UK availability)
knownFor: G6EA offers smooth power delivery but timing chain tensioner wear emerges above 70k miles
P0018 camshaft correlation code indicates jumped chain on interference engine - bent valves likely
Diesel D4HB units prized for torque but turbo oil seal leaks common at high mileage
Early second-gen units now represent strong value for budget-conscious MPV buyers
years.ticker: Kia Sedona P0018 code fix • G6EA timing chain replacement • Sedona 3.5 V6 engine for sale

years.description: Mid-cycle refresh brought minor styling updates but the G6EA 3.5L V6 remained unchanged, with timing-related failures now driving replacement demand.
keyChanges: Facelifted front/rear styling and updated interior trim across LX, SE, SR grades
No fundamental engine changes - G6EA 3.5L V6 continued with same failure patterns
Increased UK registration of high-mileage ex-fleet and taxi Sedonas entering secondary market
mainEngines: G6EA - 3.5L petrol (all trims, 195-271hp depending on tune)
knownFor: G6EA timing chain jump remains the dominant catastrophic failure mode for this period
P0300 random misfire codes often accompany timing issues due to valve timing disruption
Rebuilt G6EA units with upgraded tensioners offer cost-effective solution vs dealer pricing
Diesel D4HB units increasingly sought for towing but specialist rebuilders required
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Kia Sedona replacement engines
years.ticker: Kia Sedona 3.5 engine replacement cost • G6EA rebuilt engine UK • Sedona timing chain noise diagnosis

years.preview: Third Generation Launch: Lambda II G6DH 3.3L GDI Arrives
years.description: The all-new third generation introduced direct injection technology with the 3.3L Lambda II GDI V6, delivering more power but also new failure modes requiring specialist attention.
keyChanges: Third-generation Sedona launched with modern styling and 8-speed automatic transmission
G6DH 3.3L Lambda II GDI V6 replaced the older 3.5L MPI as the sole petrol engine
Direct injection introduced improved efficiency but new thermal management challenges
mainEngines: G6DH - 3.3L GDI petrol (LX, SE, SR, SX trims, 269-276hp)
knownFor: G6DH head bolt thread stripping from aluminium block confirmed by Kia TSB - catastrophic head gasket failure
Metal flakes on magnetic drain plug indicate progressive rod bearing wear on high-mileage units
Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves requires periodic walnut blasting maintenance
Early third-gen units now entering peak replacement window with urgent G6DH demand
years.cta: Compare rebuilt G6DH Kia Sedona engine prices
years.ticker: Kia Sedona G6DH head gasket failure • 3.3 GDI metal flakes in oil • Lambda II rod bearing replacement
enquiries: Kia Sedona G6DH head gasket failure

years.preview: Third Generation Maturity & Final Production Years
years.description: The final production years saw the G6DH 3.3L GDI refined with minor updates, but known head bolt and bearing issues persisted, driving sustained replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: Minor trim updates and equipment revisions across LX, SE, SR, SX and new Platinum grade
No fundamental engine redesign - G6DH continued with same technical specifications
Production ended 2021 with Sedona discontinued in UK market, increasing used engine demand
mainEngines: G6DH - 3.3L GDI petrol (all trims including new Platinum, 276hp final tune)
knownFor: Head gasket failure due to head bolt stripping remains the dominant G6DH failure mode across all years
Kia TSB coverage may apply to certain VIN ranges - owners should verify entitlement before paying for replacement
Rebuilt G6DH units with reinforced block threads and upgraded bearings offer permanent resolution
Discontinuation drives strong demand for verified replacement engines with warranty coverage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2021 Kia Sedona replacement engines
years.ticker: Kia Sedona 2019 engine replacement • G6DH head bolt TSB check • Sedona Platinum 3.3 GDI engine for sale
enquiries: Sedona Platinum 3.3 GDI engine for sale

faq.h2: Kia Sedona Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Kia Sedona engine?
items.answer: A rebuilt Kia Sedona engine typically costs £450-£2,200 supply-only, depending on whether you need the 3.3L G6DH or 3.5L G6EA unit. Used engines start around £400, while supply-and-fit services add £650-£1,200 labour. Prices vary by variant, mileage and supplier location across the UK network.
keyPoints: Rebuilt G6DH 3.3L GDI engines range £500-£2,200 supply-only UK
Used Kia Sedona V6 units available from £400 depending on mileage
Supply-and-fit labour typically adds £650-£1,200 to engine price
Reconditioned units include upgraded seals addressing known head bolt issues
Prices reflect current UK marketplace data from verified specialist suppliers
items.cta: Compare live Kia Sedona engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Kia Sedona?
items.answer: Replacing a Kia Sedona engine is worthwhile if the vehicle has strong service history and under 120,000 miles. With rebuilt units from £450 and the Sedona's practical 7-8 seat capacity, replacement often costs less than buying a comparable used MPV.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Kia Sedona engines cost significantly less than purchasing another large MPV
Third-generation models (2015-2021) retain strong residual value post-replacement
Supply-and-fit options available nationwide simplify the replacement process for owners
A 12-month unlimited mileage warranty protects your investment in the rebuilt unit
High-mileage vehicles over 150k miles may warrant scrapping instead of replacement
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to see if your Kia Sedona is worth saving

items.question: What are the most common Kia Sedona engine problems?
items.answer: The most reported Kia Sedona engine failures are head gasket failure on 3.3L G6DH units due to head bolt stripping, timing chain jump on 3.5L G6EA engines triggering P0018 codes, and metal flakes indicating rod bearing wear on high-mileage Lambda II V6 engines.
keyPoints: G6DH 3.3L GDI head bolt stripping causes catastrophic coolant migration into oil
G6EA 3.5L timing chain tensioner failure leads to P0018 cam correlation codes
Metal particles on magnetic drain plug signal progressive rod bearing wear on Lambda II
P0300 random misfire codes often accompany timing-related failures on interference engines
EGR cooler blockage and turbo oil seal leaks affect rare 2.2L CRDi diesel units
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Kia Sedona engine prices for your specific failure type

items.question: Which Kia Sedona engine is most reliable - 3.3 GDI or 3.5 V6?
items.answer: The 3.5L G6EA MPI V6 (2006-2014) is generally more reliable than the 3.3L G6DH GDI (2015-2021), as the newer direct-injection unit suffers from documented head bolt stripping issues confirmed by Kia TSB and owner reports.
keyPoints: G6EA 3.5L MPI engines have simpler fuel systems with fewer high-pressure components
G6DH 3.3L GDI head bolt failure is confirmed by Kia Technical Service Bulletin
Timing chain issues affect both engines but are more predictable on the 3.5L unit
Regular oil changes every 10k miles significantly extend lifespan on either V6 engine
Third-generation Sedonas with G6DH require head bolt inspection at 60k miles
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Kia Sedona 3.5L or 3.3L engine with warranty coverage

items.question: Does Kia have a TSB or warranty for Sedona head gasket failure?
items.answer: Yes, Kia issued a Technical Service Bulletin for 3.3L G6DH engines regarding head bolt stripping that can cause head gasket failure. Coverage varies by VIN, mileage and service history - contact Kia UK with your registration to check entitlement before paying for replacement.
keyPoints: Kia TSB addresses head bolt thread stripping from aluminium block on G6DH engines
Warranty coverage depends on vehicle age, mileage and documented maintenance records
Owners should request VIN-specific TSB verification from Kia UK before proceeding
Rebuilt engines from EngineMarket include upgraded bolts addressing the original design flaw
Even with TSB coverage, supply-and-fit labour costs may still apply at dealers
items.cta: Check your Kia Sedona VIN for TSB eligibility while comparing independent replacement quotes

items.question: How long should a Kia Sedona engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With regular servicing, oil changes every 10,000 miles and timely timing component replacement, Kia Sedona V6 engines can reach 150,000-180,000 miles. However, known issues like G6DH head bolt stripping may cause premature failure regardless of maintenance on third-generation models.
keyPoints: G6EA 3.5L engines often exceed 150k miles with documented service history
G6DH 3.3L GDI units may fail earlier due to head bolt design limitations
Using manufacturer-specified oil grades prevents accelerated bearing wear on Lambda II engines
Replacing timing components preventatively at 80k miles avoids catastrophic interference damage
High-mileage Sedonas benefit from compression testing before purchasing used engines
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Kia Sedona engines designed to exceed original factory lifespan

items.question: How do I find my Kia Sedona engine code before ordering a replacement?
items.answer: Your Kia Sedona engine code appears on the VIN plate under the bonnet, on the engine block near the sump, or within your V5C registration document. Common codes are G6DH for 3.3L GDI (2015-2021) and G6EA for 3.5L MPI (2006-2014).
keyPoints: Enter your registration on EnginesMarket to instantly identify your exact Sedona engine code
G6DH denotes 3.3L Lambda II GDI V6 fitted to third-generation UK Sedonas
G6EA identifies 3.5L DOHC MPI V6 used across second-generation models 2006-2014
Engine codes are stamped on the block near the oil filter housing for visual verification
Matching the correct code ensures compatibility with your Sedona's transmission and ECU
items.cta: Enter your reg now to identify your Kia Sedona engine code and get quotes

items.question: Should I buy a used, reconditioned or rebuilt Kia Sedona engine?
items.answer: Choose a rebuilt Kia Sedona engine for maximum reliability and warranty coverage, especially for G6DH units with known head bolt issues. Used engines suit tight budgets on older Sedonas, while reconditioned units offer a middle ground with upgraded seals addressing common failure points.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Kia Sedona engines include new bearings, gaskets and upgraded head bolts for G6DH
Used engines are cheapest but carry unknown history and typically shorter warranty periods
Reconditioned units undergo partial teardown with critical wear items replaced by specialists
All EngineMarket suppliers provide minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt units
Supply-and-fit services simplify installation regardless of which engine condition you select
items.cta: Compare used, reconditioned and rebuilt Kia Sedona engine prices side-by-side

items.question: Are there differences between Kia Sedona petrol and diesel engine reliability?
items.answer: Petrol V6 engines dominate the UK Kia Sedona range and are generally more reliable than the rare 2.2L CRDi diesel, which suffers from turbo oil seal leaks and EGR cooler blockage. However, petrol units have their own known issues requiring specialist attention during replacement.
keyPoints: Petrol G6EA and G6DH V6 engines power nearly all UK-market Kia Sedona models
Diesel D4HB 2.2L CRDi units are rare but sought for towing and high-mileage use
Petrol engines require attention to timing chains and head bolts; diesel to turbo and EGR
Fuel choice matters less than addressing the specific failure pattern of your Sedona's engine code
Rebuilt petrol V6 units from EngineMarket include upgrades targeting known Lambda II weaknesses
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific Kia Sedona petrol or diesel engine replacement

items.question: What should I do if my Kia Sedona has metal flakes in the oil or P0018 code?
items.answer: Metal flakes on your Kia Sedona's magnetic drain plug or a P0018 cam correlation code indicate serious internal damage requiring immediate attention. Stop driving, arrange diagnostic inspection, and compare rebuilt engine quotes - continuing to run risks catastrophic seizure or bent valves on these interference V6 engines.
keyPoints: P0018 on G6EA 3.5L signals timing chain jump - bent valves likely on interference design
Metal particles on drain plug indicate rod bearing wear requiring full Kia Sedona engine replacement
Do not add oil additives or continue driving - further damage will increase replacement cost
Rebuilt G6DH and G6EA units include upgraded bearings and timing components preventing recurrence
EngineMarket specialists can arrange collection and supply-and-fit nationwide for urgent replacements
items.cta: Get urgent quotes for Kia Sedona engine replacement if you have P0018 or metal debris
